title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Go Client Systems · Contract Triage
summary: An on-call reliability engineer tracing a production client incident must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for timeout layers, deadlines, cancellation, cleanup, and budget allocation, producing end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go 1.26 net/http end-to-end request budget and cancellation model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regres…
objectives: Separate name resolution, connect, TLS, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Propagate caller cancellation and deadlines through request creation, helpers, parsing, retries, and downstream work. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Allocate an end-to-end latency budget across attempts and phases while preserving time for cleanup and fallback. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Order the regression proof · Debug Timeout Classification
steps.instruction:
Order the Timeout Classification Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go Order the regression proof · Debug Timeout Classification.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Timeout Classification assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go Order the regression proof · Debug Timeout Classification, keep Timeout Classification connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go incident collection agent has 900 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect. Competency probe: Report which phase and policy ended an operation without exposing secrets or claiming the remote effect is known. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Timeout Classification has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Go Order the regression proof · Debug Timeout Classification, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Timeout Classification precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go Order the regression proof · Debug Timeout Classification, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before running it: Go incident collection agent has 900 ms end-to-end for DNS, connect, TLS, headers, streamed body, parse, and cleanup. A caller cancels during body read; allocate phase budgets, propagate the reason, stop owned work, and preserve unknown remote effect. Competency probe: Report which phase and policy ended an operation without exposing secrets or claiming the remote effect is known. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Order the regression proof · Debug Timeout Classification, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: All timeout errors are safely retryable and prove no server-side effect. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.

options.text: Declare Go method semantics, target authority, credentials, body ownership, and byte and time budgets for the debug Timeout Classification case
Predict Go resolution, transport, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup outcomes for the debug Timeout Classification case
Run the smallest deterministic Go request or response model and retain inspectable evidence for the debug Timeout Classification case
Inject one changed Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-network transfer limits for the debug Timeout Classification case

Competency probe: Report which phase and policy ended an operation without exposing secrets or claiming the remote effect is known. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

For Go Publish the incident record · Debug Timeout Classification, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: All timeout errors are safely retryable and prove no server-side effect. This remains false for Go 1.26 net/http.
